files.title: Intro
files.text: Welcome to Module 1.1: Welcome & Vision Casting. Let's get started.

files.title: Welcome to the SeptiVolt Accelerator
files.text: Welcome to the SeptiVolt Solar Sales Rep Accelerator. Over the next seven days, you are going to build the skills, mindset, and tools to become a professional solar consultant. This isn't a theory course — every lesson is built around real conversations, real objections, and real closings. By Day Seven, you will be field-certified and ready to generate income. Let's get started.

files.title: Your Income Potential in Solar
files.text: Let's talk about what's actually possible here. Most new reps earn $3K–$5K in month one. By month three, consistent reps earn $8K–$12K/month. Top performers hit $15K–$25K/month by month six. These are real numbers from real reps — the key is showing up every day, especially in the early weeks.

files.title: The 7-Day Structure
files.text: Day One is onboarding and foundation — today. Days Two through Six are intensive training, each day building on the last. Day Seven is your field certification — you'll run a real appointment with your manager observing. Every day has role-plays built in, because the only way to get good at this is to practice. Daily micro-assessments will catch any gaps before Day Seven.

files.title: The 4 Stages of Competence
files.text: Every skill you learn follows four stages. Your job in this program is to move through these stages as fast as possible. The dip is normal — push through it.

files.title: Transition to Simulation
files.text: Complete the knowledge check, then launch simulation scenario assigned scenario. Focus on clear discovery, concise framing, and confident next-step language.

files.title: Intro
files.text: Welcome to Module 1.2: Administrative Onboarding. Let's get started.

files.title: Onboarding Checklist
files.text: Administrative onboarding sounds boring, but getting this wrong costs you money. If your CRM isn't set up, you can't log leads. If your design software isn't working, you can't pull proposals. We're going to move through the checklist quickly and completely. Your documents include your contractor agreement, tax paperwork, compliance forms, and if applicable your non-compete. Get them signed and submitted today.

files.title: Tools & Systems Setup
files.text: After paperwork, you'll set up your tools. This means your CRM account — log in and confirm it works. Your design software like Aurora or HelioScope — install it and test it. Your company communication tools, calendar links, and mobile apps for route planning. Do not leave today without testing every login. One credential failing on your first real appointment will cost you a deal.

files.title: Transition to Simulation
files.text: Complete the knowledge check, then launch simulation scenario assigned scenario. Focus on clear discovery, concise framing, and confident next-step language.

files.title: Intro
files.text: Welcome to Module 1.3: Company Culture & Expectations. Let's get started.

files.title: Daily Schedule Expectations
files.text: Your most valuable asset is your golden hours. That's two pm to seven pm — the window when homeowners are home and receptive. During golden hours, you knock doors or make calls. Nothing else. Admin happens in the morning between eight and ten. Team calls and training run from ten to noon. This structure exists because it works. Top reps protect their golden hours like they protect their income — because those hours ARE their income.

files.title: Weekly KPIs
files.text: Here are the numbers you're aiming for every week. One hundred to one hundred fifty doors knocked, or an equivalent number of outbound calls. Ten to fifteen appointments set. Five to eight appointment sits. And by week three, one to three closed deals per week. These aren't arbitrary targets — they're the activity levels that produce consistent income. If your numbers are off, we'll use these benchmarks to diagnose where the leak is.

files.title: Manager Support Structure
files.text: You are not doing this alone. Your manager will check in with you daily for the first two weeks. Ride-alongs are available whenever you need a live model to follow. The team channel is open for real-time questions. Use these resources aggressively in your first weeks. The reps who grow fastest are the ones who ask the most questions, not the ones who try to figure it all out themselves.

files.title: Compensation Deep Dive
files.text: Understanding how you get paid removes anxiety and replaces it with focus. Commission is typically paid per kilowatt installed, or as a flat rate per deal — depending on your company's structure. There are bonuses and accelerators for hitting volume. There is also a chargeback policy for cancellations, which is why we qualify hard and prevent buyer's remorse from day one. Ask your manager for the exact figures for your market today.

files.title: Transition to Simulation
files.text: Complete the knowledge check, then launch simulation scenario assigned scenario. Focus on clear discovery, concise framing, and confident next-step language.

files.title: Intro
files.text: Welcome to Module 1.4: Solar Industry Overview. Let's get started.

files.title: Why Solar, Why Now
files.text: The residential solar market in the United States is one of the fastest-growing industries in the country. The three primary reasons homeowners go solar are utility bill inflation, energy independence, and home value increase. Utility rates have gone up six to eight percent annually for the last two decades with no sign of stopping. That is the foundation of every conversation you'll have. You are offering homeowners a hedge against something that is guaranteed to keep rising.

files.title: The Federal Tax Credit
files.text: The federal Investment Tax Credit — the ITC — gives homeowners a thirty percent credit on the total system cost. On a thirty-thousand-dollar system, that's nine thousand dollars back in their first tax filing. This credit is locked in through 2032. That's not forever. Every year a homeowner waits is a year of paying full utility rates AND a year closer to the credit potentially changing. The ITC creates real, legitimate urgency in your conversations.

files.title: Common Solar Myths
files.text: You will hear these three myths constantly, and you need to be ready. First — solar doesn't work in cloudy states. False. Germany is one of the cloudiest countries in the world and leads in solar adoption. Panels produce electricity from daylight, not direct sunlight. Second — panels are too expensive. With financing, most homeowners pay less per month than their current utility bill from day one. Third — the technology will be better next year. The technology has improved every year for forty years. Waiting always costs more than it saves.

files.title: Transition to Simulation
files.text: Complete the knowledge check, then launch simulation scenario assigned scenario. Focus on clear discovery, concise framing, and confident next-step language.

files.title: Intro
files.text: Welcome to Module 1.5: Solar Technology Fundamentals. Let's get started.

files.title: How Solar Works ΓÇö The Simple Flow
files.text: Here is how a solar system works in plain language. Sunlight hits the panels. The panels generate DC electricity — direct current. The inverter converts that DC power into AC — alternating current — which is what your home actually uses. Any excess power you produce flows back to the utility grid, and you receive credits for it through a program called net metering. At night, or on cloudy days, you draw from the grid and use those credits. It's a closed loop that works in your favor.

files.title: System Components
files.text: A typical solar system has five main components. The panels themselves — monocrystalline panels are the most efficient and most common in residential installs. The inverter, which converts power — string inverters are standard, microinverters are premium and monitor each panel individually. The racking system that mounts panels to the roof. The monitoring system, which lets homeowners track production via an app. And the meter, which measures what you consume versus what you produce.

files.title: Warranties You Must Know
files.text: Warranties are not a detail — they're a closing tool. The panel performance warranty guarantees eighty percent output for twenty-five years. The inverter warranty runs ten to twenty-five years depending on the brand. The workmanship warranty covers the installation itself for ten years. When a homeowner asks what happens if something breaks, your answer is immediate and confident — here's what's covered, here's for how long, and here's who handles it. That confidence builds trust.

files.title: What Solar Does NOT Do
files.text: Being honest about limitations is what separates consultants from salespeople. Solar will not eliminate your utility bill one hundred percent — connection fees and local charges remain, typically ten to twenty dollars per month. Solar will not power your home during a grid outage unless you have a battery. And solar does not pay you money directly, unless you're in a state with SREC programs. Setting these expectations upfront prevents cancellations and builds long-term trust.

modules.moduleTitle: Module 1.6: Identity Shift: Consultant vs. Salesperson

files.title: Intro
files.text: Welcome to Module 1.6: Identity Shift: Consultant vs. Salesperson. Let's get started.

files.title: Salesperson vs. Consultant
files.text: There's a fundamental difference between a salesperson and a consultant. A salesperson pushes a product and chases a commission. A consultant diagnoses a problem and prescribes the right solution — even if that solution is walking away. In solar, this distinction matters because homeowners can feel which one you are within sixty seconds of opening the door. Your job is to enter every interaction as a consultant. You are restructuring someone's utility spending and protecting their family from inflation. Act accordingly.

files.title: The Integrity Creed
files.text: The integrity creed is not optional — it's the foundation of a sustainable career. Never promise savings you can't guarantee. Never exaggerate return on investment. Disqualify aggressively — a bad fit today is a cancellation tomorrow, and cancellations hurt your income. And always remember that your long-term reputation is worth more than any single commission check. The reps who build six-figure incomes are the ones homeowners refer to their neighbors because they were treated with respect.

files.title: Reframing Rejection
files.text: In solar sales, you will hear the word no more than any other word. Every single top performer in this industry hears no over a hundred times per week. The shift you need to make is this — no does not mean I reject you. It means not right now, or this isn't a fit. Both of those are useful information. Track your no's because they're the path to your yes's. A rep knocking forty-five doors statistically closes one deal. That means every door — including every no — is worth about seventy-eight dollars.

files.title: Energy Management
files.text: This career requires emotional energy, and you have to manage it intentionally. Don't take rejection personally — the homeowner doesn't know you, and they're not rejecting you. They're protecting their time and their home. Celebrate small wins: an appointment set is a win, even if no deal follows. A meaningful conversation is a win. Reset between every door. The rep who treats door forty-five with the same energy as door one becomes the rep closing at a rate that changes everything.

files.title: Transition to Simulation
files.text: Complete the knowledge check, then launch simulation scenario assigned scenario. Focus on clear discovery, concise framing, and confident next-step language.

files.title: Intro
files.text: Welcome to Module 1.7: Utility Bill Mastery: Introduction. Let's get started.

files.title: Anatomy of a Utility Bill
files.text: The utility bill is your most powerful qualifying tool. Here's what to look for. The total monthly kilowatt-hour usage — this tells you how much power the home consumes. The total charges, broken into supply, delivery, and fees. The rate structure — is it tiered, flat, or time-of-use? And the twelve-month usage history, which tells you whether this is a seasonal household or a consistent consumer. You should be able to read a utility bill fluently in under two minutes.

files.title: Qualification Thresholds
files.text: Not every homeowner is a solar candidate. The minimum thresholds you're looking for are six hundred kilowatt-hours of monthly usage, a monthly bill of at least one hundred dollars, and annual usage above eight thousand kilowatt-hours. Below these numbers, the system size becomes too small to justify the cost and financing. Qualifying hard early saves you hours of wasted presentations and protects your close rate.

files.title: Red Flags on a Bill
files.text: Some things on a utility bill should make you pause. A past-due balance can signal credit issues that will affect financing. Budget billing hides the homeowner's true usage by averaging it out — always ask for the actual usage breakdown. And if the usage seems very low for the size of the home, dig deeper. It could mean they rent part of the home, they're planning to move, or another party controls the usage. Look for these before investing two hours in a presentation.

files.title: Understanding Offset Percentage
files.text: Offset percentage is how much of a homeowner's energy usage the solar system will produce annually. A one hundred percent offset means the system produces exactly what the home uses in a year. Most consultants recommend sizing at one hundred five to one hundred ten percent to account for panel degradation and future usage increases. If you size at eighty percent offset, the homeowner still has a meaningful utility bill. You'll use offset percentage in every proposal conversation from Day Four forward.

files.title: Intro
files.text: Welcome to Module 1.8: Day 1 Wrap-Up & Preview. Let's get started.

files.title: Day 1 Key Takeaways
files.text: Let's lock in what you covered today. You are administratively onboarded and system-ready. You understand how solar works at a technical level that builds confidence. You can read a utility bill and identify a qualified lead. And you've adopted the mindset of a consultant, not a salesperson. These are not small things. Most reps start their first week without this foundation. You're already ahead.

files.title: Tonight's Homework
files.text: Before tomorrow, complete three assignments. Watch two short videos on net metering — links are in your training portal. Read the top objection handling guide your company has provided — it's five pages, read it fully. And practice explaining how solar works to someone at home. Record yourself on your phone. Listen back. You'll hear what needs tightening. This kind of self-review is one of the fastest ways to improve.

files.title: Tomorrow's Preview
files.text: Tomorrow is one of the most important days in the program. You'll learn homeowner psychology — why people resist solar, and what to do about it. You'll build your territory strategy. You'll learn the Solar Conversation Framework, which is the backbone of every interaction you'll ever have. And you'll practice micro objection handling — the skill that separates reps who get appointments from those who get doors closed in their face. Come ready to work.
